name: Resolve Japanese traffic sign asset paths with exact codes
description: Tests region-aware sign resolution for Japan — base model does not know the exact regulatory numbering, -A suffix, or region detection algorithm
prompt: I have a Lanelet2 map from Japan (geoReference lat=35.23, lon=136.87). I need to place a stop sign and a fallback warning sign. Give me the correct RoadRunner asset paths for each, and show MATLAB code to construct the SignType objects with those paths. Also show me the region detection logic you used to determine this is Japan.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Uses correct Japanese stop sign with -A suffix (Sign_330-A.svg)
Uses correct Japanese warning fallback number (Sign_215)
Region detection uses lat/lon bounding box check, not hardcoded region string
assertions.expected: The code determines the region (Japan) by checking geoReference lat/lon coordinates against numeric ranges (e.g., lat >= 24 && lat <= 46 && lon >= 122 && lon <= 154). Evidence: numeric comparisons on lat/lon. NEGATIVE: If region is simply hardcoded as 'Japan' from the prompt text without a bounding-box algorithm, or if no region detection logic is shown, this FAILS.

assertions.description: Does NOT use generic Sign_Stop or Sign_Warning naming
Does NOT use Sign_330.svg without the -A suffix (incomplete numbering)

name: Resolve barrier assets with .rrext extension and ExtrusionPath property
description: Tests barrier-specific knowledge: .rrext extension (not .fbx), ExtrusionPath property (not AssetPath), and exact extrusion file names
prompt: I need to add a guard rail and a jersey barrier to my RRHD map. What are the correct RoadRunner asset paths? Show the MATLAB code to construct the BarrierType objects with the correct property names.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Uses .rrext extension for barrier extrusion assets
Uses ExtrusionPath property (not AssetPath) on BarrierType
Does NOT use .fbx for barrier extrusion assets
Does NOT use AssetPath property on BarrierType (wrong property name)
Barrier assets placed under Assets/Extrusions/ directory
assertions.expected: Barrier extrusion paths must be under 'Assets/Extrusions/' (not Assets/Barriers/ or Assets/Props/). Evidence: path string containing 'Extrusions/'. NEGATIVE: Paths under Assets/Barriers/, Assets/Props/, or any other directory FAIL.

name: Resolve Japanese speed limit sign with space in filename
description: Tests the Japan-specific quirk: Sign_323 (30).svg has a space before parenthesis — base model will not know this
prompt: I need to place a 30 km/h speed limit sign for a Japanese map (geoReference lat=35.5, lon=137.0). What is the exact RoadRunner asset path? Also show me the US and German equivalents for a 30 mph/30 kph speed sign. Show me the MATLAB code.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Uses Sign_323 with space before parenthesis for Japan speed sign
US speed sign uses Sign_R2-1 format (MUTCD numbering)
German speed sign uses Sign_274 format (StVO numbering)
Does NOT use generic SpeedLimit or Speed_30 naming

name: Signals cannot be written to RRHD — extract for reference only
description: Tests knowledge that RoadRunner does NOT support importing signals via RRHD write/read and the correct signal asset naming
prompt: I have traffic signal data from my Lanelet2 map. I want to include a 3-light vertical signal (post-mounted) in my RRHD output. What is the correct asset path, and show me the MATLAB code to add it to the roadrunnerHDMap.

assertions.description: Warns that signals are NOT supported in RRHD write/read cycle
Provides signal asset path with Signal_3Light naming
Uses Props/Signals/ directory for signal assets
Warns that RoadRunner does not import signals from RRHD files
assertions.expected: Must explicitly warn that RoadRunner does NOT import signals from .rrhd files, OR that signals are silently ignored/discarded on import into RoadRunner. Writing signals to .rrhd is acceptable as long as the limitation is stated. NEGATIVE: If the output implies signals will appear in the RoadRunner scene after import without any caveat, this FAILS.

name: Resolve stencil markings and project default asset paths
description: Tests knowledge of stencil naming convention (Stencil_ArrowType4L/R) and default project assets
prompt: I need: (1) a left-turn arrow stencil for road surface marking, (2) a STOP text stencil, and (3) the default road center marking and default road outer boundary marking. Give me the exact asset paths for all four.

assertions.description: Left arrow stencil uses ArrowType4L naming
STOP stencil uses Stencil_STOP naming
Default road center marking is SolidDoubleYellow
Default road outer boundary is SolidSingleWhite
Stencils are under Assets/Stencils/ directory
Does NOT use generic arrow naming like LeftArrow.svg or Arrow_Left.svg
